name: Jiko Raha
description: Jiko Raha is a charcoal/briquette burning improved cookstove with a built-in jacket that enables the user to simultaneously boil water.

taxonomy.sub_category: Jiko Raha Updated on November 19, 2024 · Created on October 5, 2018 Jiko Raha Jiko Raha is a charcoal/briquette burning improved cookstove with a built-in jacket that enables the user to simultaneously boil water. Developed By STAMP Investments Tested By Grand Challenges Canada’s Stars in Global Health program Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description Jiko Raha is a charcoal/briquette burning improved cookstove with a built-in jacket that enables the user to simultaneously boil water. It can sanitize approximately 3 liters of water whilst cooking with a 10 L clean water storage bag, allowing the users to have safe drinking water. STAMP Investments is in charge of the stove’s manufacturing and delivery. Target SDGs SDG 7: Affordable and Clean Energy SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation Market Suggested Retail Price $35.00 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household Distributors / Implementing Organizations STAMP Investments and Grand Challenges Canada Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include Kenyan Domestic Ceramic Jiko Stove , EcoZoom Jet , and Burn Jikokoa . Countries Botswana, Ethiopia, Kenya, Rwanda Manufacturing/Building Method The Jiko Raha is manufactured by STAMP Investments and it is made with locally available materials and manufactured by a locally trained workforce in Kenya. Intellectural Property Type Trademark User Provision Model The Jiko Raha can be obtained directly from its designer's STAMP Investments in their distribution sites or through their web page, household energy centers and NGOs like Grand Challenges Canada. Distributions to Date Status Approximately 5,000 customers have acquired the Jiko Raha. Fuel type Charcoal or briquette Chimney (yes/no) No Forced or passive Passive Pot type Round bottom Pot capacity (L) Unknown Thermal efficiency (%) Unknown PM emissions (g/MJ delivered to pot) Unknown CO emissions (g/MJ delivered to pot) Unknown Time to boil (min/L) Unknown Design Specifications The Jiko Raha is an Improved Cookstove (ICS) and it's design is composed of a vermiculite inner lining and a metal housing. The stove is equipped with a built-in jacket to purify water. Specifications: Maximum Pot Capacity: 10 L Dimensions (l/w/h): 115/115/110 mm Technical Support Provided by manufacturer. Replacement Components Not available Lifecycle 2 years Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters Unknown Vetted Performance Status Grand Challenges Canada (GCC) developed a case study on a twelve-month project where 300 cookstoves were deployed. GCC stated the key findings on Jiko Raha’s performance as: Out of the 300 households studied, 200 households (66%) continuously boiled the water using the Jiko Kenya stove every day, which exceeded the initial target of 60%. 95% of households reported an 80% reduction in the incidence of waterborne diseases, compared to the period before they used the stove. In Kitui County, the percentage of children who missed school as a result of water-related illnesses was reduced by more than 80%, due to the project. Global Innovation Exchange on their database about the Jiko Raha stated that "the stove through pilot studies was able to save a household USD 17.85 monthly with fuel reduction from USD 23.81 to only USD 5.96 monthly a 75% reduction in fuel costs and zero cost in water sanitization which has contributed to reduction of water-borne diseases." Safety The safety concerns associated with the usage of wood-burning cookstoves are primarily related with carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and sulfur dioxide emissions. Another significant safety concern is domestic accidents such as accidental burning or spill-overs. Global Innovation Exchange (GIE): Technology Platform for Development Innovations and Funding . n.d. Results for Development. Innovation Hub in the UN Visitors’ Lobby . n.d. www.sustainabledevelopment.un.org. Compliance with regulations Unknown Evaluation methods This product was evaluated for adoption, reductions in water-borne disease, fuel reductions, and impacts on school attendance. Other Information Stamp Investments has three other stove designs called Jiko Raha Institution, Jiko Kisasa, and the Uhai Stove and Jiko Ceramic Stove. The company also produces carbonized briquettes and distributes solar lanterns. Leave a Reply You must be logged in to post a comment.
